name: Muro Style Sheet

hero.title: Muro Style Sheet — A Next-Generation CSS Preprocessor
hero.summary: Muro Style Sheet (MSS) is a monochrome-first TypeScript CSS preprocessor for teams. It compiles a single .mss entry file into one output CSS bundle, providing docs-grade ergonomics, built-in theme baking, utility generation, asset rewriting, and zero-config polyfills.
features: Theme baking with light(...) and dark(...) functions
Muro Guard locks to protect brand-critical declarations
Container math helpers (cw, ch, ci, cb, cmin, cmax)
Automatic raster asset rewriting to generated .webp
Built-in module libraries for reset, grid, and typography
Watch mode for iterative development loops
Native purge, minify, and source map capabilities
privacy: Muro operates entirely as a local build tool.
No source code or telemetry data is collected during compilation.
dataUsage: Muro reads your local .mss files to generate CSS bundles.
Local raster assets are processed locally to generate .webp formats.
No data is transmitted over the internet during normal operation.
userControlSecurity: You have full control over your project assets, build outputs, and configurations.
Muro Guard ensures brand-critical rules are strictly enforced at compile time.
hero.ownershipOperator: Muro Style Sheet is developed and operated by Xerxisfy LLC. Documentation and registry packages are managed by Xerxisfy LLC.

description: Muro builds the CSS layer of a product from one clean .mss entry. It handles variables with nested scope, nesting with selector interpolation, mixins, imports, and context-aware scoped globals. The compiler replaces stacks of distinct CSS tools by providing native utility generation, asset handling, purging, and source maps out of the box.

Language & Syntax Features: Muro Guard (@lock color) for strict design token enforcement
Rich container expressions and layout shorthands
Developer Experience: muro CLI for build, watch, and purge flows
VS Code language support (.mss highlighting, comments, brackets)
Legacy .x2s file and legacy built-in migration support
Performance Optimization: Built-in AST minification and unused selector purging
Dependency-free compilation directly to raw CSS

reviews.comment: Muro removed our need for 3 different PostCSS plugins. Having utility extraction, theme baking, and asset conversion in one compiler is incredible.
Muro Guard is a game changer for adopting brand rules. Being able to lock values ensures our design system stays consistent.

Description: Install via npm: npm i -D muro-css
Add this script to package.json: "muro": "muro src/app.mss public/app.css"
Run the compiler: npm run muro
Compile a file: muro app.mss app.css
Purge unused rules: muro app.mss app.css --purge "./src"

Steps: Pass options object to map CLI functionality
Catch compilation warnings and errors natively

Description: Write expressive code that translates identically to CSS without extra tooling:
Reach out on Github for bugs, feature requests, and workflow questions.

26.0.1: Stable public release of the Muro compiler.
Finalized CLI functionality and brand package alignment (muro-css).
Shared utility generation enabled by default.
Theme baking, Guard, and built-in modules formalized.

support.documentation: Review README.md and the docs included in the repository.
support.troubleshooting: See the troubleshooting section inside the project documentation.

Core: Stand-alone node runtime execution via CLI
Bindings: Exposes compileEntry and compileString TypeScript type declarations
